lines.text: Behold, my friends! There she stands… Ragunna's Lighthouse of Dawn!
Here we are! Ragunna's dock.
You'll find Echo boats ahead to ferry you straight into the heart of Ragunna.
Well, the Carnevale hasn't started yet. We're just passing through, lending our friends a hand.
And a quiet arrival? That's not our style. We'll sail into Ragunna with flair—complete with a dazzling show and the loudest of ovations.
Don't forget to stash some confetti in your pockets for the occasion!
My pleasure! Speaking of which, where's that charming little companion of yours?
Is it asleep? The fog likely caused this. Don't worry. Echoes will recover when they're out of it.
Oh, and do yourself a favor, try our Nectarwine. They say one drop of the "Gocce di Divino" will let you rendezvous with the Sentinel in your dreams...
Ha! That might just be your shortcut to seeing the Sentinel.
May the winds be in your favor!
Raise your glasses! Shout it loud, friends!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: To us, to the Carnevale, and to our very own Laureate, {PlayerName}!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: A name that echoes through the world, destined to roam the skies before shaking the earth!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Hahaha! There you are, my friend! A compliment, if too subtle, loses all its charm!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Friends, let's keep the mood lively!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: How curious. Since when has the Primus become so interested in social calls?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Now, remember the rehearsal we did yesterday? All our preparations have led to this very moment.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Leave that to me.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Ready? The spotlight will be on you two.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Then...
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Ladies and gentlemen! The grandest and most spectacular performance of the Carnevale is about to commence!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Let us give a warm round of applause as our actors now take the stage!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: The heroes breach the first barrier, sweeping away the monsters within the city!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Now, they advance towards the Demon King's castle, where peril await at every corner... What trials lie ahead?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: The Demon King unleashes its minions, long awaiting these audacious intruders...
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Ah! A sudden twist unbalanced the heroes, then at this crucial moment, a mysterious figure enters the fray!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: A Divinity-sent emissary? Or a spirit from the shadows perhaps?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: She seems moved by their passion and righteousness,  see her join the heroes' ranks!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: It seems the Demon King anticipated the heroes' arrival and has gladly extended an invitation...
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: With unyielding determination, the heroes accept the challenge!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: The Demon King reveals itself at last, exposing its hideous visage and sharp fangs. The final showdown is nigh!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Amidst the clashing swords and the cries of the minions, the heroes face the Demon King—victory belongs to the righteous and the brave!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: And now, we await the Holy Maiden's return...
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Hear that? The thunderous roar of applause! It has been far too long! This is the true essence of the Carnevale!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Hear that? The thunderous roar of applause! It has been far too long! This is the true essence of the Carnevale!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: May I have a look?
Hmm, yes. On these seas, there is only one legend about "D."
The story of Drake, the Golden Captain.
Captain Drake, the famed hero of Rinascita. Legend has it he assembled a crew with naught but a single coin and amassed great riches from the wealthiest of men.
He then embarked on a quest to chart a safe passage across the Stormy Sea. A feat worthy of being remembered forever!
But there was a price. Only one crew member returned, and he had been driven mad. What happened out there is still a mystery.
Such tales were my bedtime stories.
Back when I just boarded the Pilgrim's Sail, with no family and nightmares that could raise the dead, Toscanini would share such stories to comfort me.
At the time, I swore that if I ever became a captain, every crew member would come back safe. No exceptions.
Something wrong, Hymer?
Bella… Since the pirates started stirring the waters around Ragunna, she's been hiring us to guard her family ships.
Well then, our next stop is clear. Fortune has a way of smiling on us, Captain. Shall we try our luck at the restaurant?
What say you? Ready to check it out?
Aye, aye, Captain! Our course is set! Ready the sails, and let the wind carry us!
How was my improvised performance, Cap? No applause or cheers for your humble bard?
Thank you! Thank you!
To be honest, I came dangerously close to it…
Alright, seems like we've got ourselves a lucky gentleman.
How about an introduction first, and then you tell us where that coin is?
Pirates?
Aldric… so it is him.
Of course! And your payment will be a one-way trip to the Order Tribunal. May you start a fresh chapter there, my friend.
An old acquaintance of mine, so to speak. We were banished on the Pilgrim's Sail together. I remember he even had a nickname—Quickfoot Aldric.
But he always had trouble embracing our ideals, so he left the troupe before long.
The next time we saw him, he had already become a pirate.
He's probably after Drake's treasures as well, which explains why he took the coin from Swiper.
Well, that's the part we'll need to see for ourselves.
Looks like we'll be burning the midnight oil. Once we arrive, we'd better keep an eye out for those plushies.
All yours, Captain!
Aldric fled like a cat with its tail on fire. Still living up to his old nickname, Quickfoot.
Don't worry, Captain. I've got a feeling our paths will cross again soon enough.
Indeed, and we reclaimed this half-coin. And next time we're in Ragunna, we'll return it to Bella.
I've got a feeling we'll cross paths with Aldric again soon enough.
Well, would you look at that. Captain, I didn't know you were fluent in Echo!
Miss, as promised, you and your friends can now dance in the moonlight to your heart's content. Go on, enjoy!
It seems Aldric spent too much time on the sea and forgot the fundamental rule of this trade: quid pro quo.
If you want the sea's rewards, you have to give something in return. Just like how being a captain doesn't just come with rights, but duties too.
Every reward has its price, and tricking his men into paying it for his own gain is absolutely unforgivable.
Anyway, take a look at this.
This map marks three isles. I'm betting they're the hiding places for Drake's treasure.
Haha, well, who could resist the siren call of Drake's fabled riches? It's tempting, no doubt.
But truth be told, it's not the treasures themselves that draw me in. It's the stories—the legend of Drake himself.
What did he face? What mysteries did he leave behind? There's no grander performance than that, Captain.
We've come this far already. I'd regret it forever if I turned back now. So yes, I want to visit those isles, and maybe we can uncover the truth about Drake's treasures.
Ha, that's exactly what I was thinking.
Pirates? No big deal. We'll deal with them like we always do.
Luckily for us, one of our troupe's Gondolas has been docked since the Carnevale. Looks like it'll come in handy after all.
Off we go! I'll lead the way.
You think so too, huh? Throwing in random plot twists like this will only confuse the audience.
Aha, so this is my hidden identity!
I'm just as curious as you are. With any luck, it'll be a catchy one for my next performance on stage.
Look at me!
Haha… Let's see how this plays out
Rescue missions!
And I didn't think you'd still be here.
Hm… where's all that treasure, then? Your crew is itching for the grand return of their selfless, heroic Captain Aldric.
Ah, I see! You want an honorable duel with my captain? A proper sea knight's duel, full of valor and—
Alright, we accept. Hand over the key.
I… I never thought he'd pick those treasures over his own life…
Back then… When Toscanini first told us about Drake's story, I said I wanted to be a captain who led the crew to countless triumphs so that everyone gets to carve out their own destiny.
Aldric just laughed, calling it foolish. He said, "You fool! With Drake's treasure, you can crush anyone and make everyone's life better by force!"
I never bought into that. For me, it's not about riches or power. It's about freedom—the freedom to sail your own course.
That's why we parted ways. He left the Troupe of Fools and became a pirate.
As a captain, you're quite different yourself.
Certainly one I'd be proud to follow.
You accept your crew for who they are, understanding and respecting their choices.
I've had my share of adventures, but I must admit—this one with you has been a breath of fresh air.
By the way, look at this.
This is Golden Captain Drake's map, a record of his life.
According to this, he and his crew did indeed brave the treacherous Stormy Sea and return with immense wealth, but not without paying dearly.
Heavy fog, disease, getting hopelessly lost, each entry could have sunk an entire fleet. By the time they made it back to the isle, only a handful survived.
Most of them never left. In the end, only one sailor remained with this coin.
Remember what that note said? "Remember the law of quid pro quo."
I believe it means if you want Drake's treasure, you have to offer something in return. The price I paid for this scroll was that coin.
Yes, but isn't it fascinating? A single coin can lead to both dreadful curses and priceless treasures.
Who would have guessed that Drake's most valuable treasure would be a map worth just one coin?
Honestly, when I made the trade, I wasn't sure what I'd get.
Hahaha! I had faith in you, my friend, and the spirit of adventure.
And this story is far more compelling than the saga of the Flaming Returned, don't you think?
Ah, you remember those pirates who said I was always on the run?
Every time a bounty was placed on my head, I'd work with my crew. They'd turn me in for the reward, and I'd handle the escape.
The rumors spread faster than I expected. Hahaha…
Anyway, if you're up for it, the trip back is long enough for me to tell you all about it.
So, Captain, the adventure's come to a close. Ready to sail back home?
It's okay.
In accordance with the law of the sea…
This is just a teeny tiny curse. Nothing more, nothing less.
But everything comes with a price, Aldric.
And now, it's time to pay yours.
Watch out, Captain!
Captain, look over there…
Looks like they're preparing for a grand gathering.
As much as I'd love to go over and stoke the fires of their fun...
Those pirates are on high alert. If our approach is clumsy, we may as well shout "Hello! We're here to ruin your party!"
Exactly what I was thinking. See those Echoes over there?
Ha, as the saying goes, "At sea, abide by the waves; on land, respect the earth."
This is a <ano=Echoes>plushie</ano> gathering, so we'd better fit in with their customs!
Come on, Captain! The night has cast its spell, and it's time for the plushies to dance.
Let's welcome the superstars of tonight's Plushie Gathering—Cuddle Wuddle and Lottie Lost!
And I'm Lottie Lost.
Because a troupe can only have one Cuddle Wuddle.
Alright, let's go give Aldric a surprise he won't forget!
Oh, right. I am the Flaming Returned, the immortal scourge of the seas. Cross me, and you'll be haunted by the crackle of flames every night!
Oh, what a generous captain you serve… truly selfless.
And yet somehow, these two were left behind to stop us?
Well then, let's head to the next isle.
It's Cetus The Tidebreaker! Are you here to see our {PlayerName} off too? Haha, I remember the moment we met {Male=him;Female=her} like it was yesterday.
Hoist the sails, O humanity! The abyss and the summit have entwined. Your only path now is forward, bold and uncharted!
You stand on the precipice. Now is the hour to decide your fate—to plant the seed of your highest hope and watch it bloom into tomorrow.
If you do not first become ashes, how will you rebuild?

Embrace your fire. Embrace your grief. Go into your solitude! I love those who dare to create, who strive to transcend, for in doing so, their old selves are destroyed. Yes, so they might reach the shores of a new life!
Oh, how I love those whose souls remain rich and vast, even when wounded. Even when the smallest risk could spell doom, they leap without hesitation into the unknown.
Should you stand alone, suffering in the endless dark... Today, may you hold fast to every last ember of courage and hope!
{PlayerName}... {PlayerName}!
Hmm... Sounds like the same thing happening to the people here.
From what I've heard, many Ragunnesi have been having peculiar dreams lately. Even some of our Troupe members too.
The details vary, but they all end the same way... the fall of Rinascita.
And it's not just in dreamland either... {PlayerName}, I'm not sure if you've heard, but there have been strange rumors appearing across Rinascita lately.
Even in Septimont, folks claim they've seen Tacet Discords not of this land in the wilds, or the ghosts of their ancestors.
But here's the odd part. Only Rinascitans seem to be having these dreams. Visitors are spared... the same should be true of you.
The Tacet Discords in the Stormy Sea have been more restless lately. And the "siren's" songs? Well, they've started sounding an awful lot like the Order's hymns...
We'll keep gathering intel on our end. Might be worth asking around the city to see who else has been experiencing this.
Rinascita's tides don't lie. Watch your back, {PlayerName}.
It appears that Pequod wasn't able to escape its fate after all...
Alas, my duty as acting captain has been fulfilled. Farewell, my friends!
Bravo! Bravo! What a magnificent duel, {PlayerName}!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Hah! I bet word of our legendary duel will spread to every corner of Septimont!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: You too, my friend!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: I'm a man blessed with endless luck, my friend! Well... perhaps a little short on strategy.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Looks like this marks the end of my Peaks of Prestige adventure.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: I gave it my all but still didn't snag that final prize Furius mentioned. But the rewards I picked up along the way? More than enough to help the Troupe live a bit easier.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Ah, that's life, isn't it? Gotta find joy in what you have!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Well then, I must dash. My charming Troupe awaits. Until next time, {PlayerName}!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige
